campaignPhases.label: Boycott & Mobilization
campaignPhases.subtitle: Late 1947 — gather veterans, evade patrols, build the movement

campaignPhases.label: The Peaceful March
campaignPhases.subtitle: 28 February 1948 — unarmed column toward Osu Castle

campaignPhases.label: Christiansborg Crossroads
campaignPhases.subtitle: 28 February 1948 — petition blocked; martyrdom at the gate

campaignPhases.label: Five-Day Accra Riots
campaignPhases.subtitle: March 1948 — youth cell, AWAM targets, truth as ammunition

missions.title: Ex-Servicemen's Oath
missions.subtitle: Old Kingsway Depot
missions.briefing: The Crown made promises in the King's name. Collect the benefit ledgers before they are burned.
missions.historicalNote: Demobilized Gold Coast veterans organize for unpaid WWII benefits, 1947.

intel.title: Ledger A
intel.text: Three million pounds owed to Gold Coast veterans — the Governor's office denies the figure.

intel.title: Ledger B
intel.text: Ex-servicemen's Union registers 2,000 members in Accra alone.

intel.title: Ledger C
intel.text: Colonial Secretary orders all benefit records classified by month's end.

asareEvent.message: Asare opens the side gate: 'I clerk nights at Kingsway. Quick — before the guard returns.'

missions.victoryText: Ledgers secured. The debt is now evidence.

missions.title: Kaneshie Lines
missions.subtitle: Market District
missions.briefing: UGCC organizers meet under the market awning. Escort them past the constabulary.
missions.historicalNote: Nationalist networks spread through Accra's markets. Nii Kwabena Bonne III's boycott movement fuels anti-colonial organizing, late 1947.

intel.title: Pamphlet Draft
intel.text: Self-government petition drafting begins — target date before dry season ends.

asareEvent.message: Asare passes a forged curfew pass. 'Show this if stopped. Trust me.'

missions.victoryText: Organizers safe. The petition lives another day.

missions.title: Radio Hill
missions.subtitle: Wireless Station
missions.briefing: Colonial troop movements are coordinated from the hill. Plant charges on both masts.
missions.historicalNote: Communications infrastructure becomes a strategic target.

asareEvent.message: Asare's note: 'Patrol shift at 0400.' Accurate intel.

missions.victoryText: Radio Hill silent. They march blind tomorrow.

missions.title: Checkpoint James Town
missions.subtitle: Coastal Road
missions.briefing: Fishermen smuggle pamphlets in fish crates. Clear James Town checkpoint for one hour.
missions.historicalNote: Coastal checkpoints tighten as nationalist activity grows.

intel.title: Sealed Orders
intel.text: Petition will be presented at Christiansborg — peaceful march, unarmed.

asareEvent.message: Asare warns of a 'special patrol' — it never arrives. Was he wrong?

missions.victoryText: Checkpoint open. Pamphlets flow to the coast.

missions.title: The Governor's Courier
missions.subtitle: Ring Road Intercept
missions.briefing: Intercept the courier. Do not kill — we need the seal intact.
missions.historicalNote: Intelligence war inside the colonial Secretariat.

intel.title: Partial Orders
intel.text: ...maintain order by all necessary means... [rest redacted — page missing]

asareEvent.message: Asare takes the satchel. One page is missing. His eyes don't meet yours.

missions.victoryText: Orders intercepted — but something doesn't add up.

missions.title: Ambush at Makola
missions.subtitle: The Betrayal
missions.briefing: Asare marks a safe corridor through Makola. Move the printing press plates tonight.
missions.historicalNote: Urban tension peaks; trust fractures within the cell.

intel.title: March Date
intel.text: March date fixed: 28 February. Assembly at Christ Church.

asareEvent.message: Only Asare knew this route. Colonial radio: 'Wrong corridor — false corridor.'

missions.victoryText: Attipoe wounded but alive. Asare's name burns in your memory.

missions.title: Shadow the Clerk
missions.subtitle: Castle Approach
missions.briefing: I no longer know whose desk Asare serves. Shadow him. You decide what happens at the gate.
missions.historicalNote: The double agent's true allegiance hangs in the balance.

witnessNodes.text: You witness Asare burn a real troop map — and hand a fake to the colonial sergeant.

asareEvent.message: Nkrumah: 'Some lies protect true marchers.' Asare was always Gold Coast.

missions.victoryText: The Clerk serves the Coast. Trust restored — barely.

missions.title: Hold Christ Church
missions.subtitle: 28 February — Dawn
missions.briefing: Thousands will gather. Hold the square until the petition is read aloud.
missions.historicalNote: Assembly at Christ Church before the march. Nearly 2,000 unarmed ex-servicemen prepare to present their petition to Governor Gerald Creasy at Christiansborg Castle.

intel.title: Petition Read
intel.text: Final petition: peaceful, loyal, demanding self-government now.

asareEvent.message: Wanted poster: Kofi Asare. Reward for arrest. He is gone — for now.

missions.victoryText: Petition read. The crowd swells toward the coast road.

missions.title: The March Column
missions.subtitle: Coast Road
missions.briefing: Unarmed. Disciplined. Visible. Escort the column down the coast road toward Osu Castle.
missions.historicalNote: The Gold Coast Regiment veterans march peacefully to present their pension petition — no weapons, only dignity.

asareEvent.message: Smoke signal on Castle wall — Asare's mark. Gate counts were falsified.

radioEvent.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
radioEvent.message: Comrades — discipline is your weapon today. Ink before iron. Hold the line with dignity; let them be the ones who strike first.

missions.victoryText: Column reaches the Crossroads. The world watches.

missions.title: Christiansborg Crossroads
missions.subtitle: 28 February 1948
missions.briefing: Present the petition. Do not provoke. Get the witnesses out — get the truth out.
missions.historicalNote: Superintendent Colin Imray and seventeen armed constables block the path. The shootings that sparked the 1948 Accra riots.

witnessNodes.text: Witness: unarmed veterans at the gate — no weapons among the marchers.
Witness: Superintendent Imray's constables open fire without warning.
Witness: the petition never reached Governor Creasy at the Castle.

asareEvent.message: Asare (radio): 'They'll arrest me at dawn. The petition copy is safe. I was always Gold Coast.'

missions.victoryText: Accra will remember. The Watson Commission will come. Independence is inevitable.

commendation.name: Witness of History
commendation.desc: Documented the Crossroads and secured the truth.

missions.epilogue: Sergeant Adjetey, Corporal Attipoe, and Private Odartey Lamptey — martyrs of Christiansborg Crossroads, 28 February 1948. Their sacrifice ignited five days of riots across Accra and set a nation on the path to independence.

missions.title: Makola Routes
missions.subtitle: 28 February 1948 — dusk
missions.briefing: Crossroads dust still on your boots. Constables sweep Makola — slip through the alleys before the cordon closes.
missions.historicalNote: The shootings at Christiansborg Crossroads scatter the city. Organizers who witnessed the martyrdom begin moving in shadow.

cellEvent.message: Kojo (whisper): 'They expect a riot. I give them a route.'

missions.victoryText: Salaga Market tonight. Find the fishmonger who won't swallow this.

missions.title: Salaga Stand
missions.subtitle: Salaga Market — AWAM shuttered
missions.briefing: Araba's cousin bleeds from Crossroads. AWAM hired constables to guard their shuttered store — hit the warehouse, not Auntie Esi's fish table.
missions.historicalNote: Nii Kwabena Bonne III's boycott closed AWAM shops across Accra. Youth cells target colonial commerce, not innocent stallkeepers.

cellEvent.message: Araba: 'You shot the men who wore your khaki. You do not get to sell us hunger anymore.'

missions.victoryText: AWAM's shutters fall. Kaneshie burns at dawn — a boy runs toward the smoke.

missions.title: Kaneshie Lines
missions.subtitle: 1 March 1948 — dawn
missions.briefing: First riot fires at the AWAM warehouse. Pull Kofi from the constables and get him to the drainage cut.
missions.historicalNote: The five-day Accra riots begin. Not every hand holds a weapon — every hand holds something: a stone, a ledger, a brother.

intel.title: AWAM Ledger
intel.text: Price-fixing records — Bonne's boycott evidence against AWAM monopoly.

intel.title: Warehouse Seal
intel.text: Colonial import stamp — proof of Crown-backed price gouging.

cellEvent.message: Kofi: 'I didn't know how to hold a rifle yesterday. I will learn today.'

missions.victoryText: Kofi runs with you. Ussher Town yard tonight — Yaw waits with roofs and rules.

missions.title: Secretariat Manifest
missions.subtitle: Victoriaborg — 2 March 1948
missions.briefing: Kwesi stole patrol manifests from the Colonial Secretariat. Switch to Kwesi (2) — he does not fight. Collect every page before the night guard returns.
missions.historicalNote: Registry clerks risked the gallows for truth. Kwesi's satchel holds curfew times, warehouse keys, and Imray's suppression order.

intel.title: Patrol Rotation
intel.text: Curfew window extended — constables thin on Ring Road after midnight.

intel.title: Warehouse Keys
intel.text: AWAM depot lock codes — weak points for the squad assault.

intel.title: Suppression Order
intel.text: Imray's signature on a order to block the petition — witness ammunition.

intel.title: Prison Transport
intel.text: Arrest schedule — Nkrumah's name circled in red.

cellEvent.message: Kwesi: 'Sorry doesn't read curfew times. This does.'

missions.victoryText: Truth is ammunition. The cell swears its chain at Yaw's yard.

commendation.name: Clerk of Conscience
commendation.desc: Recovered Colonial Secretariat manifests without bloodshed.

missions.title: Ussher Yard Stand
missions.subtitle: 5 March 1948 — the fifth day
missions.briefing: Constables sweep Ussher Town for riot organizers. Hold Yaw's yard until the Watson Commission word reaches the coast. All four must survive.
missions.historicalNote: Five days of Accra riots end; colonial inquiry follows. Independence is not this week — but the path starts here.

witnessNodes.text: Witness: youth cell documented AWAM price-fixing and Imray's suppression order.
Witness: innocent market stalls protected while AWAM warehouses burned.

cellEvent.message: Yaw: 'Four children and an old man's yard. Independence is not this week — but the path starts here.'

missions.victoryText: Accra burns five days — you burned with purpose. The Watson Commission will come.

commendation.name: Keeper of the Yard
commendation.desc: Held Ussher Town and preserved the cell's witness record.

missions.epilogue: Nkrumah will be arrested. The Gold Coast will not forget Christiansborg Crossroads — nor the youths who turned grief into evidence. Independence: 1957.
